abstract: Due to the importance of artificial intelligence (AI) in a variety of high-stakes decisions, such as loan approval, job hiring, and criminal bail, researchers in Explainable AI (XAI) have developed algorithms to provide users with recourse for an unfavorable outcome. We analyze the incentives for a decision-maker to offer recourse to a set of applicants. Does the decision-maker have the incentive to offer recourse to all rejected applicants? We show that the decision-maker only offers recourse to all applicants in extreme cases, such as when the recourse process is impossible to manipulate. Some applicants may be worse off when the decision-maker can offer recourse.

ob.ai_reason: 算法纠错激励机制，高风险AI决策与组织管理相关
ob.tldr: 研究决策者是否有意愿为被拒申请人提供算法追索权（即解释或改变结果）。通过博弈分析表明，只有在极端情况下（如追索过程不可被操纵）才会向全部申请人提供追索；在多数情形中，决策者会选择性提供，导致部分申请人反而处境更差。

title: Incentives to Offer Algorithmic Recourse
title_zh: 提供算法救济的激励机制
